risk_if_not_implemented: Without Domain-Based Message Authentication Reporting and Conformance (DMARC), unauthorized users may gain access to systems or data, increasing risk of data breaches and insider threats.

compensating_control_1.name: Email Content Protections
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to implement an email filtering security service to detect malicious attachments in emails and prevent users from accessing them.
compensating_control_1.justification: Email Content Protections (NET-20) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Domain-Based Message Authentication Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) (NET-20.4) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Phishing & Spam Protection
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize anti-phishing and spam protection technologies to detect and take action on unsolicited messages transported by electronic mail.
compensating_control_2.justification: Phishing & Spam Protection (END-08) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Domain-Based Message Authentication Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) (NET-20.4) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
